title: श्री कोदण्ड राम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
कोदण्डधारिणं रामं लक्ष्मणेन समन्वितम्।
सीताया सहितं वन्दे कोदण्डरामं सनातनम्॥१॥

दक्षिणे लक्ष्मणो यस्य वामे च जनकात्मजा।
पुरतो मारुतिर्यस्य कोदण्डरामं भजाम्यहम्॥२॥

शार्ङ्गधन्वा गदापाणिः खड्गधारी धनुर्धरः।
चक्रपाणिश्च सर्वेशः कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥३॥

ताटकान्तकरं वीरं मारीचमदनाशनम्।
शिवधनुर्भञ्जनं श्रेष्ठं कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

खरदूषणसंहारं वालिप्राणापहारकम्।
रावणान्तकरं शूरं कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

सेतुबन्धनकर्तारं लंकादहनकारकम्।
दशग्रीवविनाशार्थं कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥६॥

इक्ष्वाकुवंशतिलकं रघुनन्दनं मम्।
धर्ममूर्तिं दयासिन्धुं कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

आजानुबाहुं दीर्घाक्षं कोमलं पीतवाससम्।
मेघश्यामं विशालाक्षं कोदण्डरामं नमाम्यहम्॥८॥

कोदण्डपाणिर्भगवान् सायकैरशनिप्रभैः।
रक्षतु त्वां सदा रामः सर्वशत्रुनिबर्हणः॥९॥

कोदण्डरामस्तवनं यः पठेत् प्रातरेव हि।
रामबाणप्रभावेन सर्वशत्रुक्षयो भवेत्॥

॥ इति श्री कोदण्ड राम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Kodandadharinam Ramam Lakshmanena Samanvitam.
Sitaya Sahitam Vande Kodandaramam Sanatanam.||1||

Dakshine Lakshmano Yasya Vame Cha Janakatmaja.
Purato Marutir Yasya Kodandaramam Bhajamyaham.||2||

Sharngadhanva Gadapanih Khadgadhari Dhanurdharah.
Chakrapanishcha Sarveshah Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||3||

Tatakantakaram Viram Marichamadanashanam.
Shivadhanur-Bhanjanam Shreshtham Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||4||

Khara-Dushana-Samharam Vali-Pranapahaarakam.
Ravanantakaram Shuuram Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||5||

Setu-Bandhana-Kartaram Lanka-Dahana-Karakam.
Dashagriva-Vinashartham Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||6||

Ikshvaku-Vamsha-Tilakam Raghunandanam Mam.
Dharmamurtim Dayasindhum Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||7||

Ajanubahum Dirghaksham Komalam Pitavasasam.
Meghashyamam Vishalaksham Kodandaramam Namamyaham.||8||

Kodandapanir Bhagavan Sayakair Ashaniprabhiah.
Rakshatu Tvam Sada Ramah Sarvashatra-Nibarhanah.||9||

Kodandarama-Stavanam Yah Pathet Pratarev Hi.
Ramabana-Prabhavena Sarvashatru-Kshayo Bhavet.

|| Iti Shri Kodanda Rama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
कोदण्ड (धनुष) धारी राम, लक्ष्मण समन्वित, सीता सहित — सनातन कोदण्ड राम की मैं वन्दना करता हूँ।

दक्षिण में लक्ष्मण, वाम में जनकात्मजा, सम्मुख मारुति — ऐसे कोदण्डराम को मैं भजता हूँ।

शार्ङ्ग धनुषधारी, गदापाणि, खड्गधारी, धनुर्धर, चक्रपाणि, सर्वेश — कोदण्डराम को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

ताटका का अन्त करने वाले वीर, मारीच मद का नाश, शिवधनुष भंजन — श्रेष्ठ कोदण्डराम को नमन।

खर-दूषण संहारक, वालि के प्राण हरने वाले, रावणान्तक शूर कोदण्डराम को नमन।

सेतुबन्धन कर्ता, लंका दहन कारक, दशग्रीव विनाश हेतु कोदण्डराम को नमन।

इक्ष्वाकुवंश तिलक, रघुनन्दन, धर्ममूर्ति, दयासिन्धु कोदण्डराम को मैं नमन करता हूँ।

आजानुबाहु (जानुओं तक लम्बी भुजा), दीर्घ नेत्र, कोमल, पीतवस्त्रधारी, मेघश्याम, विशालाक्ष कोदण्डराम को नमन।

कोदण्डपाणि भगवान्, वज्रप्रभ सायकों (बाणों) से सदा तुम्हारी रक्षा करें — सर्व शत्रु निबर्हण राम।

जो प्रातःकाल कोदण्डराम स्तवन पढ़ता है, रामबाण के प्रभाव से सर्व शत्रुओं का क्षय होता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीकोदण्डरामस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य अगस्त्य ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीकोदण्डराम देवता। श्रीं रामाय नमः बीजम्। कोदण्डशक्तिः। सर्वशत्रुनाशः कीलकम्। श्रीरामप्रीत्यर्थे शत्रुनाशार्थे च पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes Rama's warrior form as the divine archer (Kodanda Rama)
Extremely powerful for destroying all enemies and opponents
Invokes protection through the power of rama's arrows
Ideal for legal battles, competitions, and facing adversaries
Recitation creates a divine shield of Rama's protection
Powerful for Dussehra, Ram Navami, and Tuesday/Sunday worship
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Rama, Kodanda Rama Stotram (श्री कोदण्ड राम स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. This revered text originates from Agastya Samhita. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to recitation creates a divine shield of Rama's protection as well as grant protection through the power of Rama's arrows. Additionally, it is known to invoke Rama's warrior form as the divine archer (Kodanda Rama). For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ram Navami and Dussehra. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Kodanda Rama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kodanda Rama Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to Rama the divine archer for protection, victory over enemies, and removal of all fears.

seoFaqs.question: What is Kodanda Rama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Kodanda Rama Stotram is a nine-verse Sanskrit hymn praising Lord Rama in his form as Kodandadhari — the wielder of the divine Kodanda bow. The word 'Kodanda' refers to Rama's celebrated bow, and 'Kodanda Rama' is the warrior aspect of Rama as the supreme archer who slays Tataka, breaks Shiva's bow at Sita's swayamvara, kills Khara-Dushana, slays Vali, and ultimately destroys Ravana with the Brahmastra. The stotra describes Rama with Lakshmana on his right, Sita on his left, and Hanuman in front — the iconic Pattabhirama-Hanuman composition.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Kodanda Rama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kodanda Rama Stotram is traditionally attributed to the Agastya Samhita, the comprehensive treatise associated with the great Vedic Rishi Agastya. The viniyog of the stotra explicitly names Agastya as the Rishi, Anushtup as the chhandas, Sri Kodanda Rama as the devata, 'Shrim Ramaya Namah' as the beeja, Kodanda Shakti as the shakti, and Sarva-shatru-nasha as the kilaka. Sage Agastya was a great devotee of Rama and is said to have presented Rama with the very Kodanda bow before his battle with Ravana.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting Kodanda Rama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti states that reciting the Kodanda Rama Stavanam in the morning brings the destruction of all enemies through the power of Rama's arrows. It is therefore especially recommended for those facing legal battles, court cases, professional adversaries, hostile competitors, and unjust opposition. Beyond enemy protection it grants courage, removes fear, creates a divine kavacha (protective shield), bestows victory in righteous causes, and invokes Rama's grace as Maryada Purushottama — the ideal of dharma, valor, and compassion.

seoFaqs.question: When should Kodanda Rama Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition recommends Sunday (Ravivar) and Tuesday (Mangalavar) for daily recitation, with special emphasis during Sri Rama Navami (Chaitra Shukla Navami), Vijayadashami / Dussehra (when Rama killed Ravana), Hanuman Jayanti, and Pushya Nakshatra days. The phalashruti specifies morning recitation (pratah) for maximum efficacy in destroying enemies. Devotees facing immediate adversity may chant it 11, 21, or 108 times in a single sitting.

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of 'Kodanda'?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Kodanda' is the proper name of Rama's divine bow — a weapon of cosmic power said to have been gifted by Rishi Agastya. The word 'Kodanda' itself refers to a powerful bow in Sanskrit. Rama wielding the Kodanda is the archetypal image of the warrior-king who upholds dharma. Kodanda Rama temples — most famously at Vontimitta in Andhra Pradesh and Tirupati's Kodandarama Temple — depict Rama in this iconic stance with bow drawn, accompanied by Sita, Lakshmana, and Hanuman.

seoFaqs.question: What deity is invoked in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The deity is Sri Kodanda Rama — Lord Rama in his bow-wielding warrior form. The stotra also venerates Sita Devi (on Rama's left), Lakshmana (on the right), and Hanuman (in front) as inseparable members of the divine darbar. Each verse references a specific heroic deed: slaying Tataka, killing Marichi, breaking Shiva's bow, slaying Khara-Dushana, killing Vali, building the Setu, burning Lanka, and destroying Ravana with the ten heads (Dashagriva).

seoFaqs.question: How many verses does Kodanda Rama Stotram contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kodanda Rama Stotram contains nine main verses (shlokas) plus a concluding phalashruti verse — totaling about ten stanzas. Each main verse ends with the refrain 'Kodandaramam Namamyaham' (I bow to Kodanda Rama), creating a powerful rhythmic devotional cadence. The complete recitation takes approximately five minutes. The chhandas (meter) is Anushtup.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Rama Stotram and Kodanda Rama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Rama Stotrams are a broad category — including Sri Rama Raksha Stotram (Budha Kaushika), Rama Hridayam, Rama Ashtottara Shatanama, Rama Sahasranama, and many bhajan-style compositions covering different aspects of Rama. The Kodanda Rama Stotram specifically focuses on Rama as the divine archer-warrior — invoking each of his battles and the power of his Kodanda bow. While Rama Raksha is recited for general protection, Kodanda Rama is chosen specifically for victory over enemies and adversarial situations.

seoFaqs.question: Can women and children recite Kodanda Rama Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes, the Kodanda Rama Stotram has no gender or age restrictions. It is open to all devotees regardless of caste, gender, or initiation status, and is widely recited in family settings. Children are often taught it as part of early Sanskrit recitation training because of its rhythmic, easy-to-memorize Anushtup meter and the powerful imagery of Rama with his bow. Sincere devotion and clear pronunciation are the only requirements.
